findings.description: No clause was found stating the obligations and rights of the controller.
findings.explanation: Art. 28(3) requires the controller's obligations and rights to be set out.
findings.recommendation: Add a clause cross-referencing the controller's responsibilities under the agreement and GDPR.
findings.title: Obligations and rights of the controller not stated

findings.description: No clause was found imposing the same data-protection obligations on subprocessors.
findings.explanation: Art. 28(4) requires the processor to impose, by contract or other legal act, the same data-protection obligations on the subprocessor as set out in the contract between the controller and processor.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Where Processor engages a Sub-processor, the same data protection obligations shall be imposed on the Sub-processor by contract.'
findings.title: Subprocessor flow-down clause missing

findings.description: No reference to SCC Clause 1 / Purpose and Scope was found.
findings.explanation: Decision 2021/914 requires Clause 1 (Purpose and Scope) — it sets out the parties' agreement to be bound by the SCCs.
findings.recommendation: Include the full Clause 1 (Purpose and Scope) text unmodified.
findings.title: SCC Clause 1 (Purpose and Scope) missing

findings.description: No reference to SCC Clause 2 / Effect and Invariability was found.
findings.explanation: Clause 2 forbids material modification — the parties may add other clauses or business-related terms only if they do not contradict the SCCs.
findings.recommendation: Include the full Clause 2 (Effect and Invariability) text unmodified.
findings.title: SCC Clause 2 (Effect and Invariability) missing

findings.description: No reference to SCC Clause 8 was found.
findings.explanation: Clause 8 contains the bulk of the Module-2 data-protection obligations (instructions, purpose limitation, transparency, accuracy, minimisation, storage limitation, security).
findings.recommendation: Include the full Clause 8 (Data Protection Safeguards) text unmodified.
findings.title: SCC Clause 8 (Data Protection Safeguards) missing

findings.description: No reference to SCC Clause 9 was found.
findings.explanation: Clause 9 governs the use of sub-processors and the prior-authorisation regime.
findings.recommendation: Include the full Clause 9 text and complete Annex III (List of Sub-processors).
findings.title: SCC Clause 9 (Use of Sub-processors) missing

findings.description: No reference to SCC Clause 14 / Transfer Impact Assessment was found.
findings.explanation: Clause 14 anchors the Schrems II Transfer Impact Assessment requirement.
findings.recommendation: Include the full Clause 14 text and document the parties' TIA in an Annex.
findings.title: SCC Clause 14 (TIA / Local Laws) missing

findings.description: No reference to SCC Clause 15 was found.
findings.explanation: Clause 15 requires the data importer to notify and challenge public-authority requests where possible.
findings.recommendation: Include the full Clause 15 text unmodified.
findings.title: SCC Clause 15 (Public Authority Access) missing

findings.description: No reference to SCC Clause 18 was found.
findings.explanation: Clause 18 sets the governing law (must be an EU Member State allowing third-party-beneficiary rights) and forum.
findings.recommendation: Include the full Clause 18 text and pick a qualifying EU Member State.
findings.title: SCC Clause 18 (Governing Law / Forum) missing

findings.description: No clause was found prohibiting use of personal information outside the specific business purpose.
findings.explanation: § 1798.140(ag)(1)(B) requires the contract to prohibit the service provider from retaining, using, or disclosing personal information for any purpose other than the specific business purpose enumerated.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Service Provider shall not retain, use, or disclose personal information for any purpose other than the specific business purpose enumerated in this Agreement.'
findings.title: CCPA purpose-limitation clause missing

findings.description: No clause was found prohibiting sale of personal information.
findings.explanation: § 1798.140(ag)(1)(A) requires a no-sale prohibition.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Service Provider is prohibited from selling personal information.'
findings.title: CCPA no-sale prohibition missing

findings.description: No clause was found prohibiting cross-context behavioral advertising.
findings.explanation: § 1798.140(ag)(1)(A) requires the contract to prohibit sharing including for cross-context behavioral advertising.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Service Provider is prohibited from sharing personal information, including for cross-context behavioral advertising.'
findings.title: CCPA cross-context-advertising prohibition missing

findings.description: No clause was found requiring the same level of privacy protection.
findings.explanation: § 1798.100(d) requires the contract to obligate the third party / service provider to comply with applicable obligations and provide the same level of privacy protection as required by the CCPA.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Service Provider shall comply with all applicable obligations under the CCPA and provide the same level of privacy protection as required by the CCPA.'
findings.title: CCPA same-level-of-protection clause missing

findings.description: No clause was found granting the business reasonable monitoring / oversight rights.
findings.explanation: § 1798.140(ag)(1)(C) requires the contract to grant the business the right to take reasonable and appropriate steps to ensure that the service provider uses the personal information in a manner consistent with the business's obligations under the CCPA.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Business may take reasonable and appropriate steps to ensure that Service Provider uses personal information in a manner consistent with Business's obligations under the CCPA.'
findings.title: CCPA monitoring right missing

findings.description: No clause was found requiring assistance with verifiable consumer requests.
findings.explanation: § 7051(a)(8) requires the contract to require the service provider to enable the business to comply with verifiable consumer requests.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Service Provider shall assist Business in responding to verifiable consumer requests under the CCPA.'
findings.title: CCPA consumer-request assistance missing

findings.description: No clause was found requiring notification if service provider can no longer meet CCPA obligations.
findings.explanation: § 7051(a)(6) requires the service provider to notify the business if it makes a determination that it can no longer meet its obligations under the CCPA.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Service Provider shall notify Business if it makes a determination that it can no longer meet its obligations under the CCPA.'
findings.title: CCPA inability-to-comply notification missing

findings.description: No clause was found requiring subcontractors to meet the same CCPA obligations.
findings.explanation: § 7051(b) requires the contract with subcontractors to include the same provisions.
findings.recommendation: Add a subcontractor flow-down requiring the same restrictions as in this Agreement.
findings.title: CCPA subcontractor flow-down missing

findings.description: No clause was found requiring written contracts with subcontractors.
findings.explanation: Every state's processor-contract statute requires subcontractor engagement to be by written contract imposing equivalent obligations.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Processor shall engage any subcontractor only pursuant to a written contract requiring the subcontractor to meet the obligations of Processor under this Agreement.'

source_citations.source: Va. Code § 59.1-579 / Colo. Rev. Stat. § 6-1-1305 / similar

findings.title: Subcontractor written-contract clause missing

findings.description: Vaulytica did not find a governing-law clause.
findings.explanation: A governing-law clause picks the legal system that interprets the contract. Without it, courts apply conflict-of-laws rules, which may produce unexpected results.
findings.title: No governing-law clause detected

findings.description: No clause was found regarding records of processing activities assistance.
findings.explanation: Art. 30 requires records of processing activities; processors typically assist controllers by providing relevant information.
findings.recommendation: Add a clause obligating the processor to provide information needed for the controller's Art. 30 RoPA.
findings.title: RoPA assistance clause missing

findings.description: No clause was found requiring DPIA assistance.
findings.explanation: Art. 35 requires a DPIA for high-risk processing; the processor is required by Art. 28(3)(f) to assist.
findings.recommendation: Add a DPIA assistance clause referencing Article 35 and the processor's role.
findings.title: DPIA assistance clause missing

findings.description: No reference to an Art. 27 EU representative was found.
findings.explanation: Art. 27 requires non-EU controllers and processors to designate an EU representative (with exceptions).
findings.recommendation: If processor is non-EU, designate and name an Art. 27 EU representative in the DPA.
findings.title: Article 27 EU representative not referenced

findings.description: No reference to a TIA was found.
findings.explanation: Schrems II / SCC Clause 14 requires parties to assess local laws and practices of the recipient country.
findings.recommendation: Reference a TIA / Transfer Risk Assessment and the parties' obligations under Clause 14 SCCs.
findings.title: Transfer Impact Assessment not referenced

findings.description: No Annex of technical and organisational measures was found.
findings.explanation: Annex II of the SCCs (and the equivalent in DPAs) lists the TOMs.
findings.recommendation: Add an Annex II describing the technical and organisational measures.
findings.title: Technical-and-organisational-measures annex missing

findings.description: No Annex listing subprocessors was found.
findings.explanation: SCC Modules 2 and 3 require Annex III listing sub-processors at the time of signature.
findings.recommendation: Add an Annex III listing approved sub-processors with the categories of data they process.
findings.title: Subprocessor list annex missing

findings.description: No governing-law clause was detected.
findings.explanation: GDPR does not specify governing law, but SCC clauses require a Member State choice; DPAs commonly mirror this.
findings.recommendation: Add a governing-law clause naming the applicable Member State (or UK) law.
findings.title: Governing-law clause missing

findings.description: No notice clause was detected.
findings.explanation: A notice clause anchors how breach notifications and other formal communications travel between the parties.
findings.recommendation: Add a notice clause naming the methods (email, certified mail), addresses, and timing requirements.
findings.title: Notice clause missing

findings.description: No survival clause was found extending obligations past termination.
findings.explanation: Where personal data is retained post-termination, the protective obligations should survive.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Obligations applicable to retained Personal Data shall survive termination.'
findings.title: Survival clause missing

findings.description: No clause was found making subprocessor-change history available to the controller.
findings.explanation: Best-practice DPAs require the processor to maintain a record of subprocessor changes for the controller.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Processor shall maintain a record of Sub-processors and changes thereto, available to Controller on request.'
findings.title: Records of subprocessor changes not referenced

findings.description: No clause was found governing onward transfers.
findings.explanation: SCC Clause 8.8 requires onward transfers to be subject to the same data protection obligations.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Onward transfers shall be subject to the same data protection obligations as set out in this Agreement.'
findings.title: Onward-transfer clause missing

findings.description: No clause was found requiring notification of public-authority requests for personal data.
findings.explanation: SCC Clauses 14 and 15 require the processor to notify the controller of legally-binding requests from public authorities and to challenge such requests where possible.
findings.recommendation: Add a clause requiring notification of public-authority requests and an obligation to challenge where permitted.
findings.title: Local-law disclosure obligation missing

findings.description: The contract does not allocate ownership of intellectual property.
findings.explanation: Without an IP-ownership clause, default copyright and patent rules apply: under 17 U.S.C. § 201, copyright vests in the author/employee unless work-for-hire or assignment applies.
findings.title: No IP-ownership clause detected

findings.description: Neither 'indemnify' nor 'hold harmless' appears in the document.
findings.explanation: Most commercial contracts allocate risk through an indemnification clause. The absence leaves the parties to default tort and contract law for any third-party claims.
findings.title: No indemnification clause detected

findings.description: Vaulytica did not find a limitation-of-liability clause.
findings.explanation: Without a limitation-of-liability clause, exposure is bounded only by what the parties can prove in damages. Most commercial contracts cap liability.
findings.title: No limitation-of-liability clause detected

findings.description: The following references do not resolve to any section: Articles 32, Articles 32.
findings.explanation: A broken cross-reference can mean the referenced section was renumbered or deleted, or that a section reference was made up. Either way the reader has no way to follow the citation.
findings.recommendation: Update each broken reference to point to the correct section, or delete it if it is no longer applicable.
findings.title: Unresolved cross-references: 2

findings.description: set forth in Annex I — Annex I is not attached to the document.

excerpt.text: ospective customers. A complete list is set forth in Annex I to this DPA.

findings.explanation: The agreement refers to an exhibit / schedule / attachment as the source of operative terms, but the referenced exhibit is missing or empty. The operative terms are therefore not in the contract. If the exhibit is meant to be supplied later, that later supplement is a new contractual document and should be governed by an amendment process.
findings.recommendation: Attach a substantive Annex I before execution, or remove the reference. If the exhibit is genuinely intended to be filled in later, mark it 'to be agreed' and add a process: who proposes, by when, what happens if the parties cannot agree.
findings.title: Annex I referenced but missing

findings.description: The contract does not state a path to terminate for material breach.
findings.explanation: Without a for-cause termination path, parties must rely on common-law material-breach doctrines, which are jurisdiction-dependent.
findings.title: No termination-for-cause clause detected

findings.description: No reference to SCC Clause 11 was found.
findings.explanation: Clause 11 obligates the data importer to inform data subjects of redress mechanisms.
findings.recommendation: Include the full Clause 11 (Redress) text unmodified.
findings.title: SCC Clause 11 (Redress) missing

findings.description: No reference to SCC Clause 16 was found.
findings.explanation: Clause 16 governs the parties' rights when the SCCs become untenable.
findings.recommendation: Include the full Clause 16 text unmodified.
findings.title: SCC Clause 16 (Non-Compliance / Termination) missing

findings.description: No fallback clause was found in case the adequacy decision is invalidated.
findings.explanation: Adequacy decisions can be invalidated (Schrems I, Schrems II). A fallback to SCCs / IDTA prevents transfer disruption.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'In the event the relied-upon adequacy decision is invalidated, the parties shall promptly implement the EU SCCs / UK Addendum / UK IDTA as a fallback.'
findings.title: Adequacy fallback clause missing

findings.description: No reference to onward-transfer terms (Clause 8.7 / 8.8) was found.
findings.explanation: SCC Clause 8.7 / 8.8 governs onward transfers to third parties outside the EEA. For the UK Addendum (layered on EU SCCs), Clause 8.8 is incorporated by reference but explicit acknowledgment is best practice.
findings.recommendation: Include the Clause 8.7 / 8.8 text or its substantive equivalent in the DPA.
findings.title: Onward-transfer terms missing

findings.description: No clause was found prohibiting combining personal information with other data sources.
findings.explanation: § 1798.140(ag)(1)(D) restricts combining the personal information with data from other sources.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Service Provider shall not combine personal information received from Business with personal information received from any other source, except as permitted by 11 CCR § 7050(c).'
findings.title: CCPA no-combining restriction missing

findings.description: No service-provider certification of CCPA understanding was found.
findings.explanation: § 7051(a)(7) expects the contract to require service-provider certification that it understands the restrictions and will comply.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Service Provider certifies that it understands the restrictions in this Agreement and the CCPA and will comply with them.'
findings.title: CCPA certification of understanding missing

findings.description: No clause was found specifically addressing sensitive personal information.
findings.explanation: CCPA / CPRA, CPA, CTDPA define sensitive personal information / sensitive data and impose heightened obligations.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'Sensitive Personal Information' clause describing categories handled and SPI-specific safeguards.
findings.title: Sensitive personal information handling not addressed

findings.description: No clause was found documenting the process for consumer rights requests (access, deletion, opt-out).
findings.explanation: All US state privacy statutes provide consumer rights; the DPA should describe the operational handoff.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'Consumer Rights Requests' clause describing intake, verification, fulfillment, and timeline.
findings.title: Consumer-rights process not documented

findings.description: No reference to data minimization was found.
findings.explanation: Several state privacy statutes incorporate a data-minimization principle requiring collection of only what is reasonably necessary.
findings.recommendation: Add: 'Personal data collected and processed shall be limited to what is reasonably necessary and proportionate to the disclosed purpose.'
findings.title: Data-minimization principle not referenced

findings.description: The document does not state where disputes must be brought.
findings.explanation: Without a venue clause, default venue rules apply (in federal court, 28 U.S.C. § 1391). A clear forum-selection clause avoids ambiguity.
findings.title: No venue / forum clause detected

findings.description: Processor shall implement and maintain appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including as appropriate: (a) encryption of personal dat

excerpt.text: Processor shall implement and maintain appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including as appropriate: (a) encryption of personal data in transit and at rest; (b) pseudonymization of personal data where possible; (c) ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems and services; (d) the ability to restore availability and access to personal data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident; (e) a process for regularly testing, assessing, and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organizational measures for ensuring the security of the processing.

findings.explanation: Triggers like 'from time to time' or 'as needed' put discretion in the hands of one party. Specify a measurable trigger when material.
findings.title: Ambiguous trigger language

findings.description: Processor shall not engage any subprocessor without prior specific or general written authorization of Controller.

excerpt.text: Processor shall not engage any subprocessor without prior specific or general written authorization of Controller.

findings.explanation: Negative covenants restrict what a party may do. Surfacing them collectively makes it easier to check they are intended and consistent with the overall deal.
findings.title: Negative covenants: 1

findings.description: Upon termination of the MSA for any reason, Processor shall, at the choice of Controller, delete

excerpt.text: 12. Deletion or Return at End of Services. Upon termination of the MSA for any reason, Processor shall, at the choice of Controller, delete or return all personal data to Controller and delete existing copies, unless applicable law requires storage of the personal data. Processor

findings.explanation: Post-termination obligations typically include return or destruction of confidential materials, deletion of data, and a certificate of destruction.
findings.title: Post-termination obligations enumerated
